description: Fix two bugs found during backend code audit: SSE stream error handling in the Node.js HTTP adapter that truncates MCP client responses, and synchronous npm subprocess call that blocks the event loop during plugin discovery.

userStories.title: Fix SSE stream error handler to destroy response instead of ending it
userStories.description: As a developer using MCP clients, I want SSE stream errors during tool dispatch to produce a transport-level error so that my client retries instead of silently receiving a truncated response.
acceptanceCriteria: In server-node.ts sendResponse(), when the ReadableStream pipe encounters an error AFTER headers have already been sent, the response socket is destroyed (res.destroy()) instead of cleanly ended (res.end())
When headers have NOT been sent yet (error before first write), the existing behavior of writing a 500 status and ending the response is preserved
The nodeStream is also destroyed on error to prevent further reads from the source

userStories.title: Replace spawnSync with async spawn for npm root -g discovery
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want plugin discovery to use async subprocess calls so that the event loop is not blocked for 50-200ms on every server startup and dev-proxy worker restart.
acceptanceCriteria: getGlobalNodeModulesPaths in resolver.ts is async (returns Promise<string[]>)
Uses execFile or spawn from node:child_process/promises instead of spawnSync
The globalThis cache is preserved — async call only runs once per process lifetime
discoverGlobalNpmPlugins properly awaits the async getGlobalNodeModulesPaths
All callers of discoverGlobalNpmPlugins already use await, so no further changes needed upstream
